claims.statement: The terrestrial lunisolar forcing anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when it remains explicitly tied to the conservative quadrupolar tide-generating potential and the inverse-cube Moon/Sun forcing relation, rather than being treated as a full ocean-tide solution.

constraints: Moon and Sun forcing must remain differential forcing relative to Earth's center
the quadrupolar tide-generating-potential form must scale with inverse cube distance
the forcing definition does not by itself certify equilibrium or dynamic tide prediction

sources.citation: Canadian Tidal Manual, sections 2.2-2.4, tide-raising force and tidal potential equations.
sources.equation: F_tide ~ 2 G M e / r^3 ; p_tide = -(G M a^2 / (2 r^3)) (3 cos^2(alpha) - 1)
sources.note: Primary source for inverse-cube tide-raising forcing and quadrupole tidal potential.
Canonical equation binding for the lane.

claims.notes: This claim is the forcing-side definition anchor only. It does not claim a certified tide solver.

claims.statement: The dynamic-tide anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when it remains explicitly distinct from the hydrostatic equilibrium-tide limit and is not promoted without a separate rotating, forced-dissipative ocean model.

constraints: dynamic tide must remain distinct from the equilibrium tide reference state
the same lunisolar forcing input may be shared without collapsing the solution semantics
no certified dynamic-tide prediction may be claimed without separate hydrodynamic modeling

sources.citation: Canadian Tidal Manual, dynamic tide discussion and equilibrium-tide limitations.
sources.note: Primary tide reference for equilibrium-vs-dynamic separation.

sources.citation: NOAA tides references, practical distinction between equilibrium tide and real tides.
sources.note: Operational reinforcement that real tides require dynamics beyond the equilibrium limit.

claims.notes: This claim exists to keep the DAG honest about maturity and model class.

claims.statement: The tidal-tensor anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when differential forcing is treated as the Hessian of the weak-field gravitational potential and not collapsed into the full hydrodynamic tide solution.

constraints: the tidal tensor must be expressed as second derivatives of the potential
the node remains a local forcing descriptor rather than a full ocean-tide solver
the lane must stay distinct from constitutive deformation response

sources.citation: Geophysical Journal International paper on inversion of gravity gradient tensor data.
sources.note: Defines the gravity-gradient/tidal tensor as second derivatives of the potential.
Canonical diagnostic equation binding.

claims.notes: This is the local forcing descriptor that feeds the response lane.

claims.statement: The Love-number anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when tidal response remains a dimensionless gain factor linking applied tidal potential to induced deformation or induced potential and is not treated as a certified interior inversion.

constraints: Love numbers must be treated as response coefficients, not direct mass-profile reconstructions
the response remains dimensionless and frequency-dependent in general
the node must stay separate from the dynamic tide and precession solver semantics

sources.citation: NASA / JPL Radio Science Techniques monograph, Love number values for planetary bodies.
sources.note: Love numbers are response coefficients and may be frequency dependent.
Canonical diagnostic equation binding.

claims.notes: This claim closes the tidal-response gain factor without over-promising a full interior inversion.

claims.statement: The equatorial-bulge torque anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when Earth-orientation response remains tied to external Moon/Sun torque on rotational oblateness and to the angular-momentum balance relation dL/dt = tau_ext.

constraints: external torque and angular momentum must be referenced about the same axis or point
the torque anchor remains a rigid-body-orientation definition rather than a full Earth-orientation series model
Moon and Sun forcing must stay distinct from fluid tide claims

sources.note: Primary Earth-science reference for Moon/Sun torque on Earth's equatorial bulge.
Generic angular-momentum balance used by the diagnostic lane.
Canonical equation binding for the torque lane.

claims.notes: This is a definitional torque anchor. It does not claim a certified precession-rate model.

claims.statement: Earth axial precession is only admissible at diagnostic tier in this lane when it is treated as spin-axis response to Moon/Sun torque on Earth's equatorial bulge and is kept explicitly distinct from orbital perihelion precession.

constraints: the claim must distinguish spin-axis precession from orbital precession
the precession lane remains downstream of the equatorial-bulge torque definition
no certified IAU Earth-orientation series may be implied by the definition anchor alone

sources.note: Primary source for Moon/Sun torque on Earth's bulge and axial precession.

sources.citation: Encyclopaedia Britannica, Precession of the Equinoxes.
sources.note: Concise reference for the precession-of-the-equinoxes mechanism.

claims.notes: This claim remains diagnostic and definitional. It is not a certified Earth-orientation time series.

claims.statement: Earth nutation is only admissible at diagnostic tier in this lane when it is treated as the shorter-period modulation of the same Moon/Sun torque-on-oblateness mechanism that drives long-term axial precession.

constraints: nutation must remain subordinate to the same torque-on-oblateness mechanism as axial precession
the shorter-period modulation must remain conceptually distinct from the long-period precession envelope
no certified Earth-orientation series or parameter solution may be implied by the definition anchor alone

sources.note: Primary source for the precession/nutation distinction.
Shared rigid-body orientation balance used by the diagnostic lane.

claims.notes: This claim remains diagnostic and mechanism-level.

claims.statement: The dynamical-ellipticity anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when H is kept distinct from J2 and is used as the orientation-relevant oblateness parameter in precession and nutation models.

constraints: H must remain the moment-of-inertia based oblateness parameter
H must be distinct from the J2 quadrupole coefficient even when the two are related
the lane remains a reduced-order orientation diagnostic rather than a full Earth-rotation model

sources.citation: Precession and nutation of the Earth depend on the Earth's dynamical flattening, H, closely related to J2 of the geopotential.
sources.note: Orientation models use H as the oblateness parameter.

sources.citation: A&A 2017, dynamical adjustments in IAU 2000A nutation arising from IAU 2006 precession.
sources.note: Shows the dynamical-ellipticity form used in precession/nutation adjustments.
Canonical diagnostic equation binding.

claims.notes: This claim keeps dynamical ellipticity as an explicit bridge quantity rather than a hidden constant.

claims.statement: The precession-constant anchor is only admissible at diagnostic tier when lunisolar torque on Earth's equatorial bulge is mediated through dynamical ellipticity and remains separate from a certified IAU precession-nutation series.

constraints: the precession constant must be expressed as a torque-over-spin-angular-momentum diagnostic quantity
the node remains downstream of dynamical ellipticity and equatorial-bulge torque
the lane does not assert a certified precession-nutation time series

sources.note: Primary Earth-orientation reference for the torque-driven precession lane.

sources.citation: Precession and nutation of the Earth depend on the Earth's dynamical flattening, H, closely related to J2 of the geopotential.
sources.note: Precession-rate models use dynamical ellipticity as the bridge variable.
Canonical diagnostic equation binding.

claims.notes: This claim records the reduced-order precession-rate bridge without elevating it to certified Earth orientation.
